family_summary: The 1971 war in East Pakistan/Bangladesh began with Operation Searchlight on the night of 25-26 March 1971, moved through a fast urban-crackdown phase, then a longer rural/guerrilla and counterinsurgency phase, and ended with a brief conventional war and surrender on 16 December 1971. Because theater, force mix, and violence mode changed sharply over time and place, the family is better analyzed as episodes rather than a single national DR estimate.
notes: Episode-first bundle. Core DR path uses explicit direct violent deaths only and keeps civilian, military/combatant, and unknown-status deaths separate.
